sections.paragraph: πληροφορέω [πλήρης, φορέω ‘bear constantly, wear’, cp. φορός ‘bringing on one’s way’] ‘reach a point at which nothing is lacking’, of events or matters of interest come to fruition Lk 1:1; of a task or responsibility carry out perfectly 2 Ti 4:5, 17; of inner or intellectual interest be totally satisfied Ro 4:21; 14:5; Col 4:12.
